slides.title: Tools, Machines, and Everyday Items
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 8: Tools, Machines, and Everyday Items. Welcome to Unit 8! This unit is all about the tools, machines, and everyday items we use at home, at school, and at work. You will learn the names of many useful objects — from a stapler and a microwave to a telescope and a helicopter. You will also learn how to describe what these things do, how to give safe instructions for using them, and how to talk about working together as a crew. By the end of the unit, you will be ready to read and enjoy this unit's mystery story, “The Attic Clue”, about a class in Rwanda who discover old tools in a dusty attic and build something amazing together. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and describe many tools, machines, and everyday items in English
Describe an object using adjectives and a relative clause with who, that, or which
Say what people have to do to stay safe when using tools and machines
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and describe many tools, machines, and everyday items in english. you will also describe an object using adjectives and a relative clause with who, that, or which. you will also say what people have to do to stay safe when using tools and machines. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: equipment, folder, briefcase, shield, stapler, hardware. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: The Right Tool for the Job
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Right Tool for the Job and A Look at the Stars. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Defining Relative Clauses (who, that, which)
bullets: A defining relative clause is an extra part of a sentence that tells us exactly which person or thing we mean. For example, in “the tool that joins...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Defining Relative Clauses (who, that, which). A defining relative clause is an extra part of a sentence that tells us exactly which person or thing we mean. For example, in “the tool that joins papers,” the words “that joins papers” tell us it is a stapler. This helps us describe the tools and machines in this unit clearly. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Describe an Everyday Item. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
